questions.text: When you tune into a podcast, what are you usually looking for — entertainment and humor to lighten your mood, substantive conversations that make you think, learning something new about a topic, industry stuff to stay sharp professionally, or just background noise while you do something else? Any specific shows that nailed what you look for?

When you crack open a book (or listen to one), what's the vibe you're after — escaping into a story and forgetting your own life for a bit, learning something that actually changes how you think or act, professional growth and skills, or just winding down before bed? Do you read different things depending on your mood, or do you stick to what you know you like?

When you're playing video games — do you ever play with friends or strangers online, or is it mostly a solo thing for you? If you do play with others, is that a big part of the appeal, or do you mostly just end up playing alone?

What's your taste in interior spaces and home aesthetics — do you have a specific vibe you gravitate toward (modern, Cozy, minimalist, eclectic), or do you just know it when you see it? Are there colors, materials, or design elements you consistently lean into or avoid?

How do you actually think about money on a gut level — is it freedom and security, a scorecard, a tool for experiences, something you don't like to think about much, or something else entirely? When you make a big purchase or financial decision, does your 'money mindset' change depending on what it's for?
